topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices.

document_1: impact of solar energy on reducing carbon emissions
document_2: benefits of energy efficiency in urban planning
document_3: role of government policies in promoting sustainability
challenges faced by the renewable energy sector

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: focus on solar and wind energy technologies
discusses the impact of renewable energy on climate change
doc2: highlights the importance of reducing carbon footprints

topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: impact of solar energy on carbon footprint
doc2: benefits of wind energy in urban areas
case studies on energy efficiency improvements

topic.name: Chronic Care Management Programs
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving care and management for individuals with chronic health conditions under Medicare and Medicaid.

Document 1: establish an integrated chronic care delivery program (Better Care Program or BCP) that promotes accountability and better care management for chronically ill patient populations
implementing through qualified BCPs strategies that prevent, delay, or minimize the progression of illness or disability associated with chronic conditions.
Document 2: provide for coverage of remote patient management services for chronic health care conditions.

topic.name: Financial Literacy and Education Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing financial literacy and education through community programs and tax incentives.

Document 1: Amends the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 to require federal financial supervisory agencies jointly to prescribe regulations establishing minimum standards for community-based financial literacy programs.
A requirement that financial institutions submit a regular report on how the institution supported and promoted financial literacy in its entire community.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the dollar limitation for the tax deduction for education loan interest to $5,000 in a taxable year for married couples filing joint tax returns.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on specific products.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on aqueous mixtures of polyvinyl alcohol and polyvinyl pyrrolidone.
Document 2: temporarily suspend the duty on certain portable slow cookers.
Document 3: modify the article description of Desmodur RF-E
extend temporarily the suspension of duty on such article.

topic.name: Military Administration and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses the administration, oversight, and legislative measures related to military personnel, mental health evaluations, and federal land management.

Document 1: Revises the definition of 'entitlement land' for which the Secretary of the Interior is required to make payments to local governments in lieu of taxes
include federal land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Defense (DOD) or the Secretary of a military department.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to establish within each military department a board of review
Defines 'covered individuals' as members and former members of the Armed Forces who have been separated from the Armed Forces since September 11, 2001.

topic.name: Indian Land Management and Leasing
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legal frameworks and regulations governing the management, leasing, and transfer of land held by federally recognized Indian tribes.

Document 1: actual rental proceeds from the lease of land acquired with a Farmers Home Administration Direct Loan by an Indian tribe
satisfy the requirement for appraisal of that land
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to transfer land the federal government holds in trust for a federally recognized Indian tribe
Allows Indian tribes to lease, or grant an easement or right-of-way on, restricted fee tribal land for any period of time without the Secretary's review and approval
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey land in the Shasta-Trinity National Forest
Requires the land acquired by the USDA Secretary to be added to and administered as part of the Six Rivers National Forest

topic.name: Veterans and Maritime Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts related to the welfare and support of veterans, maritime administration, and employee rights in federal service.

Document 1: the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to study the feasibility of entering into covered public-private partnerships to improve the access of veterans to VA medical facilities
improve the access of veterans to VA medical facilities in densely populated communities and rural communities.
Document 2: reauthorizes the U.S. Maritime Administration (MARAD) programs of the Department of Transportation (DOT) for FY2017

DOT must direct the U.S. Merchant Marine Academy to prescribe a policy and develop a program to prevent and respond to the sexual harassment and sexual assault of Academy cadets and other Academy personnel.

topic.name: Immigration and Military Service
topic.description: Legislation addressing immigration policies related to family unity and military enlistment opportunities for non-citizens.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include permanent partners
within the scope of such Act.
Document 2: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act
to restore provisions of law amended by such provision.
Document 3: Amends citizenship and residency qualifications for enlistment in the U.S. Armed Forces
to permit enlistment of additional persons who have resided continuously in a lawful status.

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at protecting public health and safety through regulation of substances and consumer products.

Document 1: amends the Controlled Substances Act to add certain synthetic opioids, hallucinogens, and cannabinoids to schedule I
subject to regulatory controls and administrative, civil, and criminal penalties
Document 2: conduct or support a comprehensive research program to determine whether exposure to electromagnetic fields from mobile communication devices causes adverse biological effects
promulgate regulations establishing maximum exposure level goals and maximum exposure levels for exposure to electromagnetic fields
Document 3: requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to identify potential safety hazards which exist in children's clothing
promulgate a consumer product safety rule establishing any necessary safety standards

topic.name: Transportation Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing safety standards and passenger rights in the transportation sector, particularly focusing on air travel and rail systems.

Document 1: Airline Passenger Bill of Rights Act of 2011 requires air carriers to submit contingency plans for passenger care during delays.
Requires public access to such plans and establishes a consumer hotline for complaints.
Document 2: Federal Railroad Safety Accountability and Improvement Act authorizes appropriations for railroad safety programs.
Urges the development of new technology to prevent loss of life and injuries at highway-rail grade crossings.
Document 3: Airline Personnel Training Enhancement Act directs regulations for training airline personnel on alcohol service and handling disruptive passengers.

topic.name: Labor and Employment Legislation
topic.description: This cluster addresses various aspects of labor laws, including child labor, collective bargaining, and unemployment insurance regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to make the restrictions on oppressive child labor inapplicable to employment of an employee age 16 or 17 by a parent.
employment of an employee age 16 or 17 by a parent, or a person standing in place of a parent, in a logging or mechanized operation owned or operated by that parent or person.
Document 2: Prohibits collective bargaining with respect to matters and questions concerning rates of basic pay of employees of the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Permits collective bargaining concerning compensation, other than basic rates of pay, with respect to such employees.
Document 3: Amends title III of the Social Security Act to require state unemployment compensation laws to require... an applicant, before receiving any UC, to: (1) complete a substance abuse risk assessment.
funding for such substance abuse testing out of current funds appropriated for the Independent Payment Advisory Board.

topic.name: Veterans and Military Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the rights, benefits, and protections of veterans and active military members.

Document 1: Prohibits any person who is mentally incapacitated from being considered adjudicated as a mental defective for purposes of the right to receive or transport firearms.
Addresses the administration of laws and benefits by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to prevent suspension of tax collection for Armed Forces members due to hospitalization for combat zone injuries.
Relates to tax equity for veterans and service members.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing opportunities and resources for small businesses, particularly those owned by veterans and socially disadvantaged individuals.

Document 1: Requires a federal agency to award to a small business concern each acquisition contract of more than $3,000 and less than $500,000
Amends the Small Business Act to prohibit considering an individual having net worth of over $1.5 million as economically disadvantaged
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to require the interagency task force to improve business development and federal contracting opportunities for small business concerns owned and controlled by veterans

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, restructuring, and services of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Authorizes the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to sell, at fair market value, to certain tax-exempt private organizations fee simple title to USPS-owned post office buildings
Requires the USPS, if a buyer under this Act makes the building available for sale, to maintain a right of first refusal to purchase the building back from the buyer
Document 2: Sets forth rules for the recalculation of annuities for employees of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS)
Establishes a Postal Innovation Advisory Commission
Document 3: Directs the U.S. Postal Service to issue a semipostal to benefit the Peace Corps

topic.name: Election Security and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the security, accessibility, and integrity of elections in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require adequate technological security measures to prevent the addresses of Members of Congress from appearing on voter registration lists.
Authorizes a Member seeking election for public office to meet state law requirements by filing an affidavit.
Document 2: Establishes the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as election days for Congress and presidential elections.
Declares the sense of Congress that state and local election officials should work together for effective weekend voting.
Document 3: Prohibits political committees from accepting Internet credit card contributions unless certain disclosure requirements are met.
Requires the treasurer of the political committee to keep an account of the contributor's name and address.

topic.name: Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts focused on health care, safety, and parental rights in medical decisions.

Document 1: Prohibits any person or organization from performing... an abortion on an unemancipated minor unless... written notification is provided to each parent
Prescribes penalties of not more than a $1 million fine and/or imprisonment for not more than 10 years for violating such prohibition.
Document 2: Amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to authorize the Administrator of FEMA to make a grant to an eligible first responder agency
Prohibits the Administrator from making a grant for costs relating to an employee being placed on active duty if federal funds are used for that employee's pay or benefits.
Document 3: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to replace a Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) program
The program must be designed to lead to full collaboration between primary care and behavioral health providers.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating financial practices to protect consumers and ensure financial stability.

Document 1: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010
protect service members and their families from abusive financial practices.
Document 3: Amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010
eliminate all supervision by the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at increasing transparency and accountability in federal agencies and their operations.

Document 3: Requires transfer of administrative jurisdiction over Coast Guard lands in Coos County, Oregon to the Secretary of the Interior.
Allows the Coast Guard to retain easements for access to aids to navigation.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumer rights in various sectors, including rental agreements, health insurance, and federal information security.

Document 1: Prescribes consumer protection guidelines for rental-purchase transactions
Grants enforcement powers to the Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act to require health plans to provide specific coverage for breast cancer treatment
Prohibits a health plan from taking specified actions to avoid the requirements of this Act
Document 3: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to issue guidance to prohibit the download, installation, or use by government employees of certain software
Directs agencies to establish or update personal use policies consistent with the guidance issued under this Act

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing social, legal, and economic issues in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes additional appropriations for Russia, Ukraine, and Belarus for humanitarian and resettlement assistance for ethnic and religious minorities.
democracy promotion; and broadcasting assistance.
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to make it unlawful for any person in the United States to knowingly transmit misleading or inaccurate caller ID information.
Provides civil and criminal penalties for violations.
Document 3: Amends federal copyright law to grant performers of sound recordings equal rights to compensation from terrestrial broadcasters.

Establishes separate annual flat fees in lieu of royalty payments for certain minority, female, religious, rural, small, noncommercial, public, educational, and community terrestrial broadcast stations.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures related to U.S. foreign policy, international relations, and national security.

Document 1: Amends the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 to authorize Department and Foreign Service special agents to investigate illegal passport or visa issuance or use.
Authorizes the Secretary to use Department uniformed guards.
Document 2: The budgets of the FSOC and the OFR are funded by assessments on financial institutions.
The OFR must submit quarterly reports to Congress regarding its finances; workforce; and actions taken to achieve the goals.
Document 3: States that it shall be U.S. policy to deny U.S. assistance to any entity or international organization that collaborates with Hamas.
Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to prohibit U.S. contributions to the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East.

topic.name: Workforce Development and Education Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing workforce skills, education programs, and the professional development of employees across various sectors.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to revise the definition of 'supervisor'
Re-empowerment of Skilled and Professional Employees and Construction Tradesworkers Act
Document 2: Establishes, revises, and extends specified science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) programs
Requires the Department of Education (DOE) to award grants for STEM secondary schools.
Document 3: Amends the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 to modify the requirements for career and technical education programs
States may develop core indicators of performance for career and technical education students at the adult education level.

topic.name: U.S. Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures related to U.S. federal regulations impacting energy, transportation, and immigration.

Document 1: Instructs the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission, the Secretary of Transportation, and the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating
require an applicant for approval under the Natural Gas Act or the Deepwater Port Act of 1974
Document 2: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide the right to pass the borders of the United States
to an American Indian born in the United States or Canada who is a member, or is eligible to be a member, of a federally recognized Indian tribe
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt from the harbor maintenance tax
Defines the 'Great Lakes Saint Lawrence Seaway System' as the waterway between Duluth, Minnesota, and Nova Scotia
